questions.text: The process through which two or more organs interact and complement the functions of one another is:
questions.explanation: Coordination ensures the harmonious functioning of various body systems.

questions.text: The structural and functional unit of the neural system is:
questions.explanation: Neurons are highly specialized cells that detect, receive and transmit different kinds of stimuli.

questions.text: Nissl's granules are found in which part of the neuron?
questions.explanation: These are granular bodies containing RNA and are involved in protein synthesis.

questions.text: Unipolar neurons, with only one axon and no dendrites, are usually found in the:
questions.explanation: Bipolar neurons are in the retina, multipolar are in the cortex, and unipolar are typical of embryos.

questions.text: The gaps between two adjacent myelin sheaths on an axon are called:
questions.explanation: These nodes allow for saltatory conduction, which is much faster than continuous conduction.

questions.text: When a neuron is not conducting any impulse (resting state), the axonal membrane is:
questions.explanation: In resting state, the membrane is relatively more permeable to Potassium ions and nearly impermeable to Sodium ions.

questions.text: The resting membrane potential is maintained by the active transport of ions by the:
questions.explanation: This pump transports 3 Na+ outwards for 2 K+ into the cell using ATP.

questions.text: Electrical synapses are rare in our system because:
options: Impulse transmission is too fast to regulate
questions.explanation: In electrical synapses, the membranes are in very close proximity, making transmission almost instantaneous and difficult to modulate.

questions.text: Which chemicals are involved in the transmission of impulses at chemical synapses?
questions.explanation: Examples include Acetylcholine and Adrenaline.

questions.text: The human brain is protected by three layers of cranial meninges. The middle layer is the:
questions.explanation: The order from outside to inside is: Dura mater, Arachnoid, and Pia mater.

questions.text: The deep cleft that divides the cerebrum longitudinally into two halves is the:
questions.explanation: The two cerebral hemispheres are connected by a tract of nerve fibers called the corpus callosum.

questions.text: The 'master clock' or the center for controlling body temperature, urge for eating and drinking is the:
questions.explanation: The hypothalamus also secretes various hormones that regulate the pituitary gland.

questions.text: The 'Limbic System' is associated with:
questions.explanation: It includes the amygdala and hippocampus and regulates emotions like excitement, pleasure, and fear.

questions.text: Which part of the brain consists of fiber tracts that interconnect different regions of the brain?
questions.explanation: Pons varolii is part of the hindbrain and acts as a bridge.

questions.text: The 'little brain' responsible for maintaining posture and balance of the body is the:
questions.explanation: The cerebellum has a very convoluted surface to provide additional space for many more neurons.

questions.text: The reflex arc consists of at least one ________ and one ________ neuron.
questions.explanation: Afferent (sensory) receives signals, and efferent (motor) transmits them to the effector organ.

questions.text: The wall of the eye ball is composed of three layers. The external layer is the:
questions.explanation: Sclera is the white, tough outer layer; the anterior portion of it is called the cornea.

questions.text: Which layer of the eye contains the photoreceptor cells?
questions.explanation: The retina contains rods (twilight vision) and cones (color/daylight vision).

questions.text: The fovea is a central pit in the yellowish pigmented spot called:
questions.explanation: The fovea is the point of greatest visual acuity (resolution) where only cones are densely packed.

questions.text: The space between the cornea and the lens is called:
questions.explanation: It contains a thin watery fluid called aqueous humor.

questions.text: The auditory ossicles (Malleus, Incus, Stapes) are found in the:
questions.explanation: They increase the efficiency of transmission of sound waves to the inner ear.

questions.text: The fluid-filled inner ear is called the:
questions.explanation: The labyrinth is divided into the bony labyrinth and the membranous labyrinth.

questions.text: The organ of Corti is located on the:
questions.explanation: It contains hair cells that act as auditory receptors.

questions.text: The vestibular apparatus is responsible for:
questions.explanation: It consists of three semi-circular canals and the otolith organ.

questions.text: Rhodopsin (visual purple) is a protein found in ________ and contains a derivative of ________.
questions.explanation: Vitamin A deficiency leads to night blindness because it's required to make retinal, a part of rhodopsin.

questions.text: The colored, opaque structure of the eye which is a visible part of the choroid is the:
questions.explanation: The iris regulates the diameter of the pupil.

questions.text: What occurs when the stimulus reaches the threshold potential in a neuron?
questions.explanation: Na+ channels open rapidly, leading to an influx of positive ions and reversal of polarity.

questions.text: The Eustachian tube connects the middle ear cavity with the:
questions.explanation: It helps in equalizing the pressures on either side of the ear drum.

questions.text: The association areas in the cerebral cortex are responsible for:
questions.explanation: These areas are neither clearly sensory nor motor in function.

questions.text: The saltatory conduction of nerve impulse occurs in:
questions.explanation: The impulse 'jumps' from one node of Ranvier to the next.

questions.text: Which part of the brain is the main thinking part?
questions.explanation: Cerebrum is the largest part and handles complex functions like reasoning and memory.

questions.text: Corpora quadrigemina are found in the:
questions.explanation: These are four round swellings (lobes) on the dorsal portion of the midbrain.

questions.text: The space between the lens and the retina is the:
questions.explanation: It is filled with a transparent gel called vitreous humor.

questions.text: At the blind spot:
questions.explanation: It is the point where the optic nerves leave the eye; no image is formed here.

questions.text: Daylight (photopic) vision and color vision are functions of:
questions.explanation: Cones come in three types, sensitive to red, green, and blue light.

questions.text: The medulla contains centers which control:
questions.explanation: The medulla is vital for involuntary life-sustaining functions.

questions.text: Wax secreting glands in the ear are called:
questions.explanation: Cerumen (earwax) helps protect the ear canal.

questions.text: In myelinated nerve fibers, Schwann cells form the:
questions.explanation: Schwann cells wrap around the axon to provide insulation.

questions.text: The part of the ear that acts as a hearing organ is the:
questions.explanation: The cochlea converts sound vibrations into neural impulses.

questions.text: Which system is often called 'the fight or flight' system?
questions.explanation: The sympathetic nervous system prepares the body for stressful or emergency situations.
